reasoning: Based on searches up to May 6, 2025: While China recently launched major ongoing military drills ("Strait Thunder-2025A") around Taiwan in early April 2025 — which included "rehearsal blockades" the US condemned as destabilizing — there is no evidence the United States has formally accused the PRC of actually blockading a Taiwanese seaport without consent. Taiwan categorizes an actual blockade as an act of war. A formal US accusation of a true blockade would mean a massive international crisis has already begun, which is not the case as of early May 2025. Given the short two-month horizon until July 1, 2025, and China’s historical preference for gray-zone maneuvers and rehearsal drills rather than precipitating a full kinetic war or total actual blockade in the current climate, the probability of an actual blockade occurring and being formally accused by the US by July 1 is very low.
